item.title: KDDI Email System Breach Exposes Up to 14.2 Million ISP Logins
item.content:encoded:
Data breach exposes up to 14.2 million email logins at six ISPs — Must Read

KDDI says attackers exploited a third-party software flaw in one of its email systems, potentially exposing email addresses and passwords for up to 14.2 million current, former, and inactive customers across six Japanese ISPs. Affected users are being urged to reset passwords and enable 2FA where available.

  • KDDI Corporation disclosed a data breach in which threat actors accessed one of its email systems used by five other Japanese internet service providers.
  • KDDI discovered the compromise on June 17 and said it blocked the attacker and implemented defensive measures.
  • The company said attackers exploited a vulnerability in unnamed third-party software used on its system.
  • The incident may have exposed email addresses and passwords for up to 14.2 million current, former, and inactive customers, including inactive accounts.
  • KDDI advised potentially affected customers to reset their email account passwords and enable two-factor authentication if available.
